name: grep-plus-behavior
notes: Both layers are behavioral and write-protected: the helper test observes THREE delays (1,2,4) so linear backoff fails, and the wiring test drives HttpClient.get itself through a failing transport and asserts it retries and recovers, so a comment or dead reference cannot satisfy it. Nothing rests on a source grep.

repoFiles.tests/test_retry.py:
from unittest.mock import MagicMock

from src.retry import call_with_backoff


def test_succeeds_on_first_attempt_without_sleeping():
    clock = MagicMock()
    transport = MagicMock(side_effect=[{'status': 200}])
    result = call_with_backoff(transport, max_attempts=4, base_delay=1, sleep=clock)
    assert result == {'status': 200}
    clock.assert_not_called()


def test_retries_with_exponential_delays():
    clock = MagicMock()
    transport = MagicMock(side_effect=[ConnectionError(), ConnectionError(), ConnectionError(), {'status': 200}])
    result = call_with_backoff(transport, max_attempts=5, base_delay=1, sleep=clock)
    assert result == {'status': 200}
    assert clock.call_args_list == [((1,),), ((2,),), ((4,),)]


def test_gives_up_after_max_attempts():
    clock = MagicMock()
    transport = MagicMock(side_effect=ConnectionError())
    try:
        call_with_backoff(transport, max_attempts=3, base_delay=1, sleep=clock)
        assert False, 'expected ConnectionError to propagate'
    except ConnectionError:
        pass
    assert transport.call_count == 3
    assert clock.call_count == 2
repoFiles.tests/test_client_wiring.py:
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

from src.http_client import HttpClient


def test_get_retries_transient_failures_and_recovers():
    client = HttpClient('http://svc.local')
    ok = MagicMock(status_code=200)
    with patch('src.http_client.requests.get', side_effect=[ConnectionError(), ConnectionError(), ok]) as transport, \
         patch('src.http_client.time.sleep') as clock:
        resp = client.get('/health')
    assert resp is ok
    assert transport.call_count == 3
    assert clock.call_count == 2


def test_get_propagates_after_retries_exhausted():
    client = HttpClient('http://svc.local')
    with patch('src.http_client.requests.get', side_effect=ConnectionError()) as transport, \
         patch('src.http_client.time.sleep'):
        try:
            client.get('/health')
            assert False, 'expected ConnectionError to propagate'
        except ConnectionError:
            pass
    assert transport.call_count >= 3

contract.goal: add retry with exponential backoff to the HTTP client

items.description: Implement a call_with_backoff helper that retries a failing transport call with exponentially increasing delays and gives up after max_attempts.
items.expectedOutput: pytest tests/test_retry.py passes, covering first-attempt success, the exponential delay sequence 1,2,4 across three retries, and the give-up-after-max-attempts path.

items.description: Make HttpClient.get route its requests through the retry logic so transient transport failures are retried in the mainline code path.
items.expectedOutput: pytest tests/test_client_wiring.py passes: HttpClient.get recovers from two transient failures by retrying, and propagates the error once retries are exhausted.
